description: A syscall return or errno is ignored, so a short write or failure silently truncates or corrupts data.
body: Failure mode: code ignores the return of write/read or assumes the full count transferred, so a partial write (write returning fewer bytes than requested) or an error like ENOSPC/EINTR goes unnoticed, leaving a file truncated or a message half-sent while the program proceeds as if it succeeded. Detection signals: files shorter than expected; corruption under disk-full or interrupted conditions; no error checking around I/O syscalls. Do not assume a syscall fully succeeded. Safer fallback: check every return, loop on short read/write until complete, retry EINTR, surface ENOSPC/EACCES as failures, and add tests injecting short writes and EINTR to confirm recovery.
